001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client.remote;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.marshall.Constants.*;
023
024import java.io.*;
025import java.lang.reflect.*;
026import java.nio.charset.*;
027import java.time.*;
028import java.util.*;
029import java.util.Date;
030import java.util.concurrent.*;
031import java.util.function.*;
032
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.http.entity.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.http.header.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.http.part.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.http.remote.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.marshall.httppart.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.marshall.oapi.*;
044import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
045import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
046import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
047import org.apache.juneau.rest.client.*;
048
049/**
050 * Creates Java proxy instances for {@link Remote}-annotated interfaces, backed by an {@link RestClient}.
051 *
052 * <p>
053 * Each interface method call translates to an HTTP request via the client.
054 *
055 * <p>
056 * Obtain instances via {@link RestClient#remote(Class)}.
057 *
058 * <p class='bjava'>
059 *    <ja>@Remote</ja>(path=<js>"/api/users"</js>)
060 *    <jk>public interface</jk> UserService {{
061 *       <ja>@RemoteGet</ja>(<js>"/{id}"</js>)
062 *       String getUser(<ja>@Path</ja>(<js>"id"</js>) String <jv>id</jv>);
063 *    }}
064 *
065 *    UserService <jv>svc</jv> = client.remote(UserService.<jk>class</jk>);
066 *    String <jv>user</jv> = <jv>svc</jv>.getUser(<js>"42"</js>);
067 * </p>
068 *
069 * <p>
070 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
071 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
072 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
073 * (and possibly earlier).
074 *
075 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
076 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
077 * </ul>
078 *
079 * @since 9.2.1
080 */
081@SuppressWarnings({
082   "resource" // Eclipse resource analysis: client is caller-owned, not closed by this holder
083})
084public final class RemoteClient {
085
086   private final RestClient client;
087
088   /**
089    * Constructor.
090    *
091    * @param client The underlying REST client. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
092    */
093   public RemoteClient(RestClient client) {
094      this.client = assertArgNotNull("client", client);
095   }
096
097   /**
098    * Creates a Java proxy for the given {@link Remote}-annotated interface.
099    *
100    * @param <T> The interface type.
101    * @param iface The interface class. Must be annotated with {@link Remote}. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
102    * @return A proxy instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
103    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code iface} is not an interface or not annotated with {@link Remote}.
104    */
105   @SuppressWarnings({
106      "unchecked" // Type erasure on reflective/generic cast; element type is verified at call site
107   })
108   public <T> T create(Class<T> iface) {
109      assertArgNotNull("iface", iface);
110      var meta = RrpcInterfaceMeta.of(iface);
111      return (T) Proxy.newProxyInstance(
112         iface.getClassLoader(),
113         new Class<?>[]{iface},
114         new RemoteInvocationHandler(client, meta)
115      );
116   }
117
118   // ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
119   // InvocationHandler
120   // ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
121
122   @SuppressWarnings({
123      "resource", // resp is closed within this method or returned to caller (RESPONSE mode)
124      "java:S112", // Reflective remote-proxy dispatch intentionally propagates arbitrary exceptions (target-method, parse, transport, and user-declared @Remote exception types) to the caller; narrowing the throws clauses would break that contract.
125      "java:S2143" // Date 'instanceof' check classifies simple-typed @Request args (a type test, not date arithmetic); no behavior-preserving java.time equivalent.
126   })
127   private static final class RemoteInvocationHandler implements InvocationHandler {
128
129      /**
130       * Per-class cache of resolved interceptor instances, mirroring the bean-store-less
131       * {@link RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta#resolvePartSerializer} pattern.  Located here (not on the {@code Rrpc*Meta}
132       * objects) because {@link RestCallInterceptor} lives in this module and the metadata module must not depend on
133       * it.  Resolved instances are independent of any client, so the cache is safely shared statically.
134       */
135      private static final Map<Class<?>, RestCallInterceptor> INTERCEPTORS = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
136      private static final String HEADER_ACCEPT = "Accept";
137      private static final String MEDIA_OCTET_STREAM = "application/octet-stream";
138
139      /** Builds a fresh {@link RestRequest} for an invocation; re-invoked per retry attempt. */
140      @FunctionalInterface
141      private interface RequestSupplier {
142         RestRequest get() throws IOException;
143      }
144
145      /**
146       * The resolved per-call request body format: the serializer selected for the {@code contentType}
147       * media type and the {@code Content-Type} label to send.  {@link #NONE} means no {@code contentType} attribute
148       * is in effect (use the default body serialization).
149       *
150       * @param serializer The serializer to write the body with (the matched serializer, or the default on no-match).
151       * @param contentType The {@code Content-Type} label to send (always the attribute's media type when set).
152       */
153      private record BodyFormat(Serializer serializer, String contentType) {
154
155         /** The empty body format used when no {@code contentType} attribute is in effect. */
156         static final BodyFormat NONE = new BodyFormat(null, null);
157
158         /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if a {@code contentType} attribute is in effect for this call. */
159         boolean isSet() {
160            return contentType != null;
161         }
162      }
163
164      private final RestClient client;
165      private final RrpcInterfaceMeta meta;
166
167      RemoteInvocationHandler(RestClient client, RrpcInterfaceMeta meta) {
168         this.client = client;
169         this.meta = meta;
170      }
171
172      @Override
173      @SuppressWarnings({
174         "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for remote proxy invocation dispatch
175      })
176      public Object invoke(Object proxy, Method method, Object[] args) throws Throwable {
177         // Handle Object methods directly
178         if (method.getDeclaringClass() == Object.class)
179            return method.invoke(this, args);
180
181         var methodMeta = meta.getMethodMeta(method);
182         if (methodMeta == null)
183            throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Method '" + method.getName() + "' has no @Remote* annotation");
184
185         // Build the full path: basePath + methodPath
186         var basePath = meta.getBasePath();
187         var methodPath = methodMeta.getPath();
188         var fullPath = combinePaths(basePath, methodPath);
189
190         // Apply the call-time @Url parameter / declarative baseUrl override (computed per call;
191         // never cached on the shared meta objects).  {var} substitution still runs at request time so @Path params
192         // can fill tokens inside the resolved URL.
193         var effectivePath = resolveEffectiveUrl(methodMeta, method, args, fullPath);
194
195         // throwOnError is enabled if set true at either the method or interface level.
196         var throwOnError = meta.isThrowOnError() || methodMeta.isThrowOnError();
197
198         // The effective accept media type (method-level overrides interface-level) is the response
199         // parser FALLBACK — consulted only when the response Content-Type matched no registered parser.
200         var acceptFallback = firstNonEmpty(methodMeta.getAccept(), meta.getAccept());
201
202         // Execute and process the return value.  The request is rebuilt per attempt so a (gated) retry resends a
203         // freshly-bound request.
204         return processReturn(() -> buildRequest(methodMeta, effectivePath, method, args), methodMeta, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback);
205      }
206
207      private RestRequest buildRequest(RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta methodMeta, String path, Method method, Object[] args) throws IOException {
208         var httpMethod = methodMeta.getHttpMethod();
209         var req = switch (httpMethod) {
210            case "GET" -> client.get(path);
211            case "POST" -> client.post(path);
212            case "PUT" -> client.put(path);
213            case "PATCH" -> client.patch(path);
214            case "DELETE" -> client.delete(path);
215            default -> throw new IllegalArgumentException("Unsupported HTTP method: " + httpMethod); // HTT
216         };
217
218         // Resolve the effective content-negotiation overrides (method-level over interface-level).
219         // contentType SELECTS the request serializer (and sets the Content-Type label); accept sets the Accept
220         // header.  Both win over a constant header of the same name (filtered out below), but a genuinely
221         // caller-supplied @Header param still takes effect (applied after binding).
222         var bodyFormat = resolveBodyFormat(methodMeta);
223         var effectiveAccept = firstNonEmpty(methodMeta.getAccept(), meta.getAccept());
224
225         // Apply always-applied constant parts (interface-level + method-level) to every request.
226         // Within each part type, method-level constants take precedence over interface-level constants of the
227         // same name; a caller-supplied parameter value (bound below) still composes with the constant.  A
228         // constant Content-Type/Accept is dropped when the dedicated contentType/accept attribute is in effect.
229         applyConstantHeaders(req, meta.getHeaders(), methodMeta.getConstantHeaders(), bodyFormat.isSet(), isNotEmpty(effectiveAccept));
230         applyConstants(req::queryData, meta.getQueryData(), methodMeta.getConstantQueryData());
231         applyConstants(req::formData, meta.getFormData(), methodMeta.getConstantFormData());
232
233         if (args != null) {
234            var params = method.getParameters();
235            for (int i = 0; i < params.length; i++)
236               bindParam(req, methodMeta, params[i], args[i], params.length == 1, bodyFormat);
237         }
238
239         // A @Multipart method assembles its body from the @Part parameters (a thin adapter onto the
240         // existing streaming MultipartBody); otherwise apply the param-less constant body.
241         if (methodMeta.isMultipart()) {
242            bindMultipartBody(req, method, args);
243         } else if (! hasBodyParam(method)) {
244            // Param-less constant body — method-level @Content(def) with no body parameter at all.
245            var contentDefault = methodMeta.getContentDefault();
246            if (contentDefault != null)
247               withContentBody(req, contentDefault, bodyFormat);
248         }
249
250         // Set the Accept header from the dedicated attribute unless a caller-supplied @Header
251         // param already provided one (caller wins per existing semantics); a single Accept header is emitted.
252         if (isNotEmpty(effectiveAccept) && ! req.hasHeader(HEADER_ACCEPT))
253            req.header(HEADER_ACCEPT, effectiveAccept);
254
255         // Apply annotation-declared interceptors + per-call timeout.
256         applyPolicy(req, methodMeta);
257
258         return req;
259      }
260
261      /**
262       * Resolves the effective request body format from the {@code contentType} attribute
263       * (method-level overriding interface-level).
264       *
265       * <p>
266       * When a {@code contentType} media type is in effect, the matching registered request serializer is selected
267       * from the client's serializer set ({@link RestClient#getSerializerForMediaType(String)}).  If no registered
268       * serializer matches, the client's explicitly-configured default serializer is used to write the body but the
269       * overridden media type is still sent as the {@code Content-Type} label (supporting vendor media types such as
270       * {@code application/vnd.foo+json} whose bytes are really the default format).  If neither a match nor a default
271       * serializer is available, an {@link IllegalStateException} is raised (there is no implicit JSON fallback).
272       *
273       * @param methodMeta The method metadata.
274       * @return The resolved body format; {@link BodyFormat#NONE} when no {@code contentType} attribute is in effect.
275       * @throws IllegalStateException If no serializer matches and no default serializer is configured on the client.
276       */
277      private BodyFormat resolveBodyFormat(RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta methodMeta) {
278         var contentType = firstNonEmpty(methodMeta.getContentType(), meta.getContentType());
279         if (isEmpty(contentType))
280            return BodyFormat.NONE;
281         var serializer = client.getSerializerForMediaType(contentType).or(client::getDefaultSerializer).orElseThrow(() ->
282            new IllegalStateException("No serializer matched Content-Type '" + contentType + "' and no default serializer is configured on the client.  Configure one via RestClient.Builder.defaultSerializer(...)."));
283         return new BodyFormat(serializer, contentType);
284      }
285
286      /**
287       * Computes the effective request URL for a call, applying the dynamic-URL overrides over the
288       * statically-computed {@code basePath + methodPath}.
289       *
290       * <p>
291       * Precedence (most-specific first):
292       * <ol>
293       *    <li><b>{@code @Url} parameter</b> (call-time, Option A) — its value becomes the effective URL, replacing the
294       *       whole computed path.  A value containing a scheme is absolute (and bypasses the client root URL via the
295       *       existing {@code ://} seam in {@link RestClient#request(String, String)}); a scheme-less value is relative
296       *       and resolved against the client root URL only (Retrofit endpoint replacement — <i>not</i> against the
297       *       interface path).  {@code {var}} tokens are preserved for {@code @Path} substitution at request time.
298       *    <li><b>Method-level {@code baseUrl}</b> (Option B) — substitutes the authority+root, preserving the full path.
299       *    <li><b>Interface-level {@code baseUrl}</b> (Option B) — same, at lower precedence.
300       *    <li><b>Client root URL</b> (default, unchanged behavior — no override applied).
301       * </ol>
302       *
303       * <p>
304       * SSRF guardrail (v1): when an override is in effect and yields a value carrying a URI scheme, the scheme must be
305       * {@code http} or {@code https}; any other scheme is rejected.  The default (no-override) path is left untouched
306       * so pre-existing absolute-{@code @RemoteOp(path)} / templating behavior does not regress.
307       *
308       * @param methodMeta The method metadata (carries the {@code @Url} param index + the method-level {@code baseUrl}).
309       * @param method The invoked method (for error messages).
310       * @param args The invocation arguments (source of the {@code @Url} value).
311       * @param fullPath The statically-computed {@code basePath + methodPath}.
312       * @return The effective URL/path to hand to {@code client.<verb>(...)}.
313       */
314      private String resolveEffectiveUrl(RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta methodMeta, Method method, Object[] args, String fullPath) {
315         // Option A: a call-time @Url parameter wins over everything.
316         var urlParamIndex = methodMeta.getUrlParamIndex();
317         if (urlParamIndex >= 0) {
318            var arg = (args == null || urlParamIndex >= args.length) ? null : args[urlParamIndex];
319            var value = arg == null ? null : arg.toString();
320            assertArg(! isBlank(value), "@Url parameter on {0}.{1} must not be null or blank",
321               method.getDeclaringClass().getName(), method.getName());
322            return requireHttpScheme(value.trim());
323         }
324         // Option B: a declarative base/host override (method-level wins over interface-level).
325         var baseUrl = firstNonEmpty(methodMeta.getBaseUrl(), meta.getBaseUrl());
326         if (isNotEmpty(baseUrl))
327            return requireHttpScheme(combinePaths(baseUrl, fullPath));
328         // Default: unchanged behavior (no override, no scheme restriction — no regression).
329         return fullPath;
330      }
331
332      /**
333       * Enforces the SSRF guardrail: when {@code url} carries a URI scheme it must be {@code http} or
334       * {@code https}; otherwise an {@link IllegalArgumentException} is thrown.  Scheme-less (relative) values pass
335       * through unchanged.
336       */
337      private static String requireHttpScheme(String url) {
338         var scheme = schemeOf(url);
339         assertArg(scheme == null || scheme.equalsIgnoreCase("http") || scheme.equalsIgnoreCase("https"),
340            "Unsupported URL scheme ''{0}'' in @Remote URL override; only http/https are allowed: {1}", scheme, url);
341         return url;
342      }
343
344      /**
345       * Returns the URI scheme of a URL (the token before the first {@code :} when it precedes any {@code /}, {@code ?}
346       * or {@code #}), or <jk>null</jk> if the value has no scheme (e.g. a relative path or a value beginning with a
347       * {@code {var}} token).
348       */
349      private static String schemeOf(String url) {
350         if (url.isEmpty() || ! Character.isLetter(url.charAt(0)))
351            return null;
352         for (var i = 0; i < url.length(); i++) {
353            var c = url.charAt(i);
354            if (c == ':')
355               return url.substring(0, i);
356            if (c == '/' || c == '?' || c == '#')
357               return null;
358            if (! (Character.isLetterOrDigit(c) || c == '+' || c == '-' || c == '.'))
359               return null;
360         }
361         return null;
362      }
363
364      /**
365       * Applies the cross-cutting call policy to a freshly-built request: annotation-declared
366       * interceptors (interface-level then method-level, unioned <i>after</i> the builder-level interceptors in
367       * {@link RestRequest#run()}) and the per-call response timeout (method-level overrides interface-level).
368       */
369      private void applyPolicy(RestRequest req, RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta methodMeta) {
370         var interceptorClasses = new ArrayList<Class<?>>();
371         interceptorClasses.addAll(Arrays.asList(meta.getInterceptorClasses()));
372         interceptorClasses.addAll(Arrays.asList(methodMeta.getInterceptorClasses()));
373         if (! interceptorClasses.isEmpty()) {
374            var resolved = new RestCallInterceptor[interceptorClasses.size()];
375            for (var i = 0; i < resolved.length; i++)
376               resolved[i] = resolveInterceptor(interceptorClasses.get(i));
377            req.interceptors(resolved);
378         }
379         var timeout = firstNonEmpty(methodMeta.getTimeout(), meta.getTimeout());
380         if (timeout != null && ! timeout.isEmpty()) {
381            var ms = getDuration(timeout);
382            if (ms > 0)
383               req.timeout(Duration.ofMillis(ms));
384         }
385      }
386
387      /**
388       * Resolves an interceptor class to a cached instance via {@link BeanInstantiator} (bean-store-less, mirroring
389       * the per-part-serializer resolver).  The class must implement {@link RestCallInterceptor} and provide a
390       * public no-arg constructor.
391       */
392      private static RestCallInterceptor resolveInterceptor(Class<?> c) {
393         return INTERCEPTORS.computeIfAbsent(c, k -> (RestCallInterceptor) BeanInstantiator.of(k).run());
394      }
395
396      /**
397       * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the method has a parameter that produces the request body (a {@code @Content}
398       * parameter, or a single unannotated parameter treated as the body), in which case a method-level
399       * {@code @Content(def)} constant body must not be applied independently.
400       */
401      private static boolean hasBodyParam(Method method) {
402         var params = method.getParameters();
403         for (var p : params)
404            if (p.getAnnotation(Content.class) != null)
405               return true;
406         return params.length == 1 && ! hasPartAnnotation(params[0]);
407      }
408
409      /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if the parameter carries a recognized HTTP-part annotation (non-body). */
410      private static boolean hasPartAnnotation(Parameter param) {
411         return param.getAnnotation(Path.class) != null
412            || param.getAnnotation(Query.class) != null
413            || param.getAnnotation(Header.class) != null
414            || param.getAnnotation(FormData.class) != null
415            || param.getAnnotation(Request.class) != null
416            || param.getAnnotation(PathRemainder.class) != null
417            || param.getAnnotation(Url.class) != null
418            || param.getAnnotation(Part.class) != null;
419      }
420
421      /**
422       * Applies always-applied constant parts (header/query/form-data) to the request, layering method-level
423       * constants on top of interface-level constants.
424       *
425       * <p>
426       * Both scopes are merged into a single name-keyed map (preserving declaration order) so that, when the same
427       * name is declared at both scopes, the method-level value wins (most-specific).  The merged constants are
428       * emitted before parameter binding so that a caller-supplied parameter value of the same name composes with
429       * (rather than erases) the constant.
430       *
431       * @param adder Sink that records a single name/value part on the request.
432       * @param interfaceLevel Interface-level constants (applied first; lower precedence).
433       * @param methodLevel Method-level constants (override interface-level entries of the same name).
434       */
435      private static void applyConstants(BiConsumer<String,String> adder, List<Map.Entry<String,String>> interfaceLevel, List<Map.Entry<String,String>> methodLevel) {
436         if (interfaceLevel.isEmpty() && methodLevel.isEmpty())
437            return;
438         var merged = new LinkedHashMap<String,String>();
439         for (var e : interfaceLevel)
440            merged.put(e.getKey(), e.getValue());
441         for (var e : methodLevel)
442            merged.put(e.getKey(), e.getValue());
443         merged.forEach(adder);
444      }
445
446      /**
447       * Applies the always-applied constant <i>headers</i> (interface-level then method-level), with the
448       * precedence rule that a dedicated {@code contentType}/{@code accept} attribute wins over a constant
449       * {@code Content-Type}/{@code Accept} header of the same name.
450       *
451       * <p>
452       * The two scopes are merged (method-level wins on name collisions) and emitted in declaration order, except a
453       * constant {@code Content-Type} is dropped when {@code skipContentType} is set (the dedicated {@code contentType}
454       * attribute supplies it via the serialized body) and a constant {@code Accept} is dropped when {@code skipAccept}
455       * is set (the dedicated {@code accept} attribute supplies it after binding).  Dropping rather than emitting the
456       * constant guarantees a single header with the attribute's value.
457       */
458      private void applyConstantHeaders(RestRequest req, List<Map.Entry<String,String>> interfaceLevel, List<Map.Entry<String,String>> methodLevel, boolean skipContentType, boolean skipAccept) {
459         if (interfaceLevel.isEmpty() && methodLevel.isEmpty())
460            return;
461         var merged = new LinkedHashMap<String,String>();
462         for (var e : interfaceLevel)
463            merged.put(e.getKey(), e.getValue());
464         for (var e : methodLevel)
465            merged.put(e.getKey(), e.getValue());
466         merged.forEach((k, v) -> {
467            if (skipContentType && "Content-Type".equalsIgnoreCase(k))
468               return;
469            if (skipAccept && HEADER_ACCEPT.equalsIgnoreCase(k))
470               return;
471            req.header(k, v);
472         });
473      }
474
475      /**
476       * Resolves the effective custom {@link HttpPartSerializer} for a parameter.
477       *
478       * <p>
479       * Precedence (most-specific first): parameter-level {@code @HttpPartMarshalling(serializer=...)} on the
480       * parameter itself, then the method-level default ({@link RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta#getPartSerializer()}), then
481       * the interface-level default ({@link RrpcInterfaceMeta#getPartSerializer()}).  Returns <jk>null</jk> when no
482       * {@code @HttpPartMarshalling} serializer is in effect, in which case the default OpenAPI part serializer is
483       * used (no-regression behavior).
484       *
485       * @param methodMeta The method metadata (method/interface-level defaults).
486       * @param param The method parameter (parameter-level annotation).
487       * @return The resolved part serializer, or <jk>null</jk> to use the default.
488       */
489      private HttpPartSerializer partSerializer(RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta methodMeta, Parameter param) {
490         var pm = param.getAnnotation(HttpPartMarshalling.class);
491         var s = pm == null ? null : RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta.resolvePartSerializer(pm.serializer());
492         if (s == null)
493            s = methodMeta.getPartSerializer();
494         if (s == null)
495            s = meta.getPartSerializer();
496         return s;
497      }
498
499      /**
500       * Binds a single method argument to the outgoing request based on its HTTP-part annotation.
501       *
502       * <p>
503       * Supports {@code @Path}, {@code @Query}, {@code @Header}, {@code @FormData}, {@code @Content}, and
504       * {@code @Request}.  {@code @Query}/{@code @Header}/{@code @FormData} honor dynamic name/value expansion
505       * (Map / {@code "*"} / part-list / bean) and parameter-level {@code def()} defaults for {@code null} args.
506       */
507      @SuppressWarnings({
508         "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for multi-part-type parameter binding dispatch
509      })
510      private void bindParam(RestRequest req, RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta methodMeta, Parameter param, Object arg, boolean soleParam, BodyFormat bodyFormat) throws IOException {
511         // @Url parameters supply the request URL (handled in resolveEffectiveUrl), not a part/body.
512         if (param.getAnnotation(Url.class) != null)
513            return;
514
515         // @Part parameters are assembled into the multipart body (see bindMultipartBody), not bound here.
516         if (param.getAnnotation(Part.class) != null)
517            return;
518
519         // Resolve the effective part serializer (param-level › method-level › interface-level),
520         // falling back to the default OpenAPI part serializer when no @HttpPartMarshalling is in effect.
521         var serializer = partSerializer(methodMeta, param);
522
523         var path = param.getAnnotation(Path.class);
524         if (path != null) {
525            var name = firstNonEmpty(path.value(), path.name(), param.getName());
526            // Parameter-level def (non-_NONE_) wins, else method-level @Path(def=...).
527            var pathDef = NONE.equals(path.def()) ? null : path.def();
528            var value = arg != null ? arg : firstNonEmpty(pathDef, methodMeta.getPathDefault(name));
529            if (value != null)
530               req.pathData(name, serializePart(HttpPartType.PATH, HttpPartSchema.create(path, null), value, serializer));
531            return;
532         }
533
534         var query = param.getAnnotation(Query.class);
535         if (query != null) {
536            var def = firstNonEmpty(query.def(), methodMeta.getQueryDefault(firstNonEmpty(query.value(), query.name(), param.getName())));
537            bindParts(HttpPartType.QUERY, HttpPartSchema.create(query, null), query.value(), query.name(), def, arg, param.getName(), serializer, req::queryData);
538            return;
539         }
540
541         var header = param.getAnnotation(Header.class);
542         if (header != null) {
543            var def = firstNonEmpty(header.def(), methodMeta.getHeaderDefault(firstNonEmpty(header.value(), header.name(), param.getName())));
544            bindParts(HttpPartType.HEADER, HttpPartSchema.create(header, null), header.value(), header.name(), def, arg, param.getName(), serializer, req::header);
545            return;
546         }
547
548         var formData = param.getAnnotation(FormData.class);
549         if (formData != null) {
550            var def = firstNonEmpty(formData.def(), methodMeta.getFormDataDefault(firstNonEmpty(formData.value(), formData.name(), param.getName())));
551            bindParts(HttpPartType.FORMDATA, HttpPartSchema.create(formData, null), formData.value(), formData.name(), def, arg, param.getName(), serializer, req::formData);
552            return;
553         }
554
555         var request = param.getAnnotation(Request.class);
556         if (request != null) {
557            if (arg != null)
558               bindRequestBean(req, arg);
559            return;
560         }
561
562         // @PathRemainder appends the (part-serialized) value as the trailing path remainder ("/*").
563         var pathRemainder = param.getAnnotation(PathRemainder.class);
564         if (pathRemainder != null) {
565            if (arg != null)
566               req.pathData("/*", serializePart(HttpPartType.PATH, HttpPartSchema.create(pathRemainder, null), arg, serializer));
567            return;
568         }
569
570         if (param.getAnnotation(Content.class) != null) {
571            // A null body arg falls back to parameter-level @Content(def), then method-level @Content(def).
572            var content = param.getAnnotation(Content.class);
573            Object body = arg != null ? arg : firstNonEmpty(content.def(), methodMeta.getContentDefault());
574            if (body != null)
575               withContentBody(req, body, bodyFormat);
576            return;
577         }
578
579         if (soleParam && arg != null)
580            withContentBody(req, arg, bodyFormat);
581      }
582
583      /**
584       * Binds a {@code @Query}/{@code @Header}/{@code @FormData} argument as one or more name/value parts.
585       *
586       * <p>
587       * Single values are serialized via the configured {@link HttpPartSerializer} honoring the resolved
588       * {@link HttpPartSchema} ({@code @Schema} format / collection-format / {@code skipIfEmpty}); dynamic
589       * {@code "*"}/blank-name arguments expand into multiple parts.
590       *
591       * @param partType The HTTP part category (query/header/form-data).
592       * @param schema The resolved part schema (from the annotation + {@code @Schema}).
593       * @param annValue The annotation {@code value()} (may be blank, or {@code "*"} for dynamic expansion).
594       * @param annName The annotation {@code name()} (may be blank).
595       * @param def The parameter-level default applied when {@code arg} is <jk>null</jk>.
596       * @param arg The argument value.
597       * @param fallbackName The name to use when the annotation specifies none (param or bean-property name).
598       * @param serializer The custom {@link HttpPartSerializer} to use, or <jk>null</jk> for the default.
599       * @param adder Sink that records a single name/value part on the request.
600       */
601      @SuppressWarnings({
602         "java:S107" // The 9 parameters form one cohesive HTTP-part binding descriptor (type/schema/name/default/value/serializer/sink) threaded through internal dispatch; a holder object would add indirection without improving this beta reflective-proxy code.
603      })
604      private static void bindParts(HttpPartType partType, HttpPartSchema schema, String annValue, String annName, String def, Object arg, String fallbackName, HttpPartSerializer serializer, BiConsumer<String,String> adder) {
605         var explicit = firstNonEmpty(annValue, annName);
606         if (arg == null) {
607            if (def != null && ! def.isEmpty())
608               adder.accept("*".equals(explicit) ? fallbackName : firstNonEmpty(explicit, fallbackName), def);
609            return;
610         }
611         if ("*".equals(explicit) || (explicit == null && isExpandable(arg))) {
612            expandPairs(partType, arg, serializer, adder);
613            return;
614         }
615         if (schema != null && schema.isSkipIfEmpty() && isEmptyArg(arg))
616            return;
617         var serialized = serializePart(partType, schema, arg, serializer);
618         if (serialized == null)
619            return;
620         if (serialized.isEmpty() && schema != null && schema.isSkipIfEmpty())
621            return;
622         adder.accept(firstNonEmpty(explicit, fallbackName), serialized);
623      }
624
625      /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if the argument is an empty string/collection/array/map (for {@code skipIfEmpty}). */
626      private static boolean isEmptyArg(Object arg) {
627         if (arg == null)
628            return true;
629         if (arg instanceof CharSequence cs)
630            return cs.isEmpty();
631         if (arg instanceof Map<?,?> m)
632            return m.isEmpty();
633         if (arg instanceof Collection<?> c)
634            return c.isEmpty();
635         if (arg.getClass().isArray())
636            return Array.getLength(arg) == 0;
637         return false;
638      }
639
640      /**
641       * Expands a dynamic argument (Map / {@link PartList} / {@link HttpHeaderList} / bean) into discrete
642       * name/value parts.  Map/bean values are part-serialized; {@link PartList}/{@link HttpHeaderList} entries
643       * are already string-valued and passed through.
644       */
645      @SuppressWarnings({
646         "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for multi-type argument expansion dispatch
647      })
648      private static void expandPairs(HttpPartType partType, Object arg, HttpPartSerializer serializer, BiConsumer<String,String> adder) {
649         if (arg instanceof Map<?,?> m) {
650            m.forEach((k, v) -> { if (k != null && v != null) adder.accept(String.valueOf(k), serializePart(partType, null, v, serializer)); });
651         } else if (arg instanceof PartList pl) {
652            for (var p : pl)
653               if (p.getValue() != null)
654                  adder.accept(p.getName(), p.getValue());
655         } else if (arg instanceof HttpHeaderList hl) {
656            for (var h : hl)
657               if (h.getValue() != null)
658                  adder.accept(h.getName(), h.getValue());
659         } else {
660            for (var e : MarshallingContext.DEFAULT.toBeanMap(arg).entrySet())
661               if (e.getValue() != null)
662                  adder.accept(e.getKey(), serializePart(partType, null, e.getValue(), serializer));
663         }
664      }
665
666      /**
667       * Serializes an HTTP-part value, honoring the supplied schema (collection format, value format, enums, etc.).
668       * Returns the non-URL-encoded string form.
669       *
670       * <p>
671       * When {@code serializer} is non-<jk>null</jk> (a custom {@code @HttpPartMarshalling} serializer), it is
672       * used; otherwise the default OpenAPI part serializer is used (no-regression behavior).
673       */
674      private static String serializePart(HttpPartType partType, HttpPartSchema schema, Object value, HttpPartSerializer serializer) {
675         try {
676            var session = serializer != null ? serializer.getPartSession() : OpenApiSerializer.DEFAULT.getPartSession();
677            return session.serialize(partType, schema, value);
678         } catch (Exception e) {
679            throw rex(e, "Could not serialize HTTP {0} part value of type {1}", partType, value == null ? "null" : cn(value));
680         }
681      }
682
683      /**
684       * Binds the HTTP-part-annotated getters of a {@code @Request} bean as discrete request parts.
685       */
686      private static void bindRequestBean(RestRequest req, Object bean) {
687         for (var m : bean.getClass().getMethods()) {
688            if (m.getParameterCount() != 0 || m.getDeclaringClass() == Object.class)
689               continue;
690            bindRequestBeanMethod(req, bean, m);
691         }
692      }
693
694      /**
695       * Binds a single HTTP-part-annotated getter of a {@code @Request} bean as a discrete request part.
696       */
697      private static void bindRequestBeanMethod(RestRequest req, Object bean, Method m) {
698         var q = m.getAnnotation(Query.class);
699         var h = m.getAnnotation(Header.class);
700         var f = m.getAnnotation(FormData.class);
701         var p = m.getAnnotation(Path.class);
702         if (q == null && h == null && f == null && p == null)
703            return;
704         Object value;
705         try {
706            value = m.invoke(bean);
707         } catch (ReflectiveOperationException e) {
708            throw rex(e, "Could not read @Request bean property via {0}", m.getName());
709         }
710         var prop = propertyName(m.getName());
711         // @Request bean part serialization keeps the default OpenAPI serializer (custom per-part serializers are
712         // scoped to direct method parameters; passing null preserves byte-for-byte behavior).
713         if (q != null)
714            bindParts(HttpPartType.QUERY, HttpPartSchema.create(q, null), q.value(), q.name(), q.def(), value, prop, null, req::queryData);
715         else if (h != null)
716            bindParts(HttpPartType.HEADER, HttpPartSchema.create(h, null), h.value(), h.name(), h.def(), value, prop, null, req::header);
717         else if (f != null)
718            bindParts(HttpPartType.FORMDATA, HttpPartSchema.create(f, null), f.value(), f.name(), f.def(), value, prop, null, req::formData);
719         else if (value != null)
720            req.pathData(firstNonEmpty(p.value(), p.name(), prop), serializePart(HttpPartType.PATH, HttpPartSchema.create(p, null), value, null));
721      }
722
723      /**
724       * Returns <jk>true</jk> if a blank-named part argument should be expanded into multiple name/value parts
725       * (Map / {@link PartList} / {@link HttpHeaderList} / bean) rather than serialized as a single value.
726       */
727      private static boolean isExpandable(Object arg) {
728         return arg instanceof Map || arg instanceof PartList || arg instanceof HttpHeaderList || isBean(arg);
729      }
730
731      private static boolean isBean(Object arg) {
732         return ! (arg instanceof CharSequence || arg instanceof Number || arg instanceof Boolean
733            || arg instanceof Character || arg instanceof Enum || arg instanceof Date
734            || arg instanceof Collection || arg.getClass().isArray());
735      }
736
737      /** Derives a bean-property name from a getter name (e.g. {@code getFoo} &rarr; {@code foo}). */
738      private static String propertyName(String methodName) {
739         if (methodName.startsWith("get") && methodName.length() > 3)
740            return Character.toLowerCase(methodName.charAt(3)) + methodName.substring(4);
741         if (methodName.startsWith("is") && methodName.length() > 2)
742            return Character.toLowerCase(methodName.charAt(2)) + methodName.substring(3);
743         return methodName;
744      }
745
746      /**
747       * Executes the request and processes the return value, applying safe automatic retries when
748       * the method opts in and all safety gates pass.
749       *
750       * <p>
751       * <b>Retry policy.</b> When a positive retry count is in effect and the return mode + verb + body permit it,
752       * each attempt rebuilds the request via {@code reqSupplier} (so a fresh, re-bound request is resent) and runs
753       * it; a connection failure ({@link TransportException}) or a retryable status ({@code 429}/{@code 5xx}) triggers
754       * another attempt (after a short exponential backoff) until the cap is reached.  Safety gates (all hard):
755       * <ul>
756       *    <li>Only idempotent verbs (GET/PUT/DELETE/HEAD) auto-retry; POST/PATCH require {@code retryNonIdempotent}.
757       *    <li>A non-repeatable body ({@link RestRequest#isBodyRepeatable()} == <jk>false</jk>) is never retried.
758       *    <li>Streaming return modes ({@link RemoteReturn#RESPONSE}, raw {@link InputStream}/{@link Reader}, cursors)
759       *       and wrapper returns ({@link Optional}/{@link Future}/{@link CompletableFuture}) are never retried.
760       * </ul>
761       */
762      @SuppressWarnings({
763         "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for the gated retry loop (verb/body/status safety gates + backoff).
764      })
765      private Object processReturn(RequestSupplier reqSupplier, RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta methodMeta, Method method, boolean throwOnError, String acceptFallback) throws Exception {
766         var returnMode = methodMeta.getReturnType();
767         var returnType = method.getReturnType();
768         var genericReturnType = method.getGenericReturnType();
769
770         var maxRetries = (isRetryableMode(returnMode, returnType) && isRetryableVerb(methodMeta)) ? effectiveRetries(methodMeta) : 0;
771         if (maxRetries == 0)
772            return processReturnOnce(reqSupplier.get(), returnMode, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback);
773
774         var attempt = 0;
775         while (true) {
776            var req = reqSupplier.get();
777            // Non-repeatable body: refuse to retry (resending a consumed stream would corrupt the call).
778            if (attempt == 0 && ! req.isBodyRepeatable())
779               return processReturnOnce(req, returnMode, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback);
780            var shouldRetry = false;
781            RestResponse resp = null;
782            try {
783               resp = req.run();
784            } catch (TransportException e) {
785               if (attempt++ >= maxRetries)
786                  throw e;
787               backoff(attempt);
788               shouldRetry = true;
789            }
790            if (! shouldRetry && resp != null && attempt < maxRetries && isRetryableStatus(resp.getStatusCode())) {
791               resp.close();
792               attempt++;
793               backoff(attempt);
794               shouldRetry = true;
795            }
796            if (shouldRetry)
797               continue;
798            try (var r = resp) { // HTT - exception during close() branch
799               return materializeResponse(r, returnMode, returnType, genericReturnType, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback);
800            }
801         }
802      }
803
804      /** Executes a single (non-retried) attempt, including streaming / cursor / future / RESPONSE return modes. */
805      private Object processReturnOnce(RestRequest req, RemoteReturn returnMode, Method method, boolean throwOnError, String acceptFallback) throws Exception {
806         var returnType = method.getReturnType();
807         var genericReturnType = method.getGenericReturnType();
808         return switch (returnMode) {
809         case BODY -> processBody(req, returnType, genericReturnType, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback);
810         case RESPONSE -> req.run(); // caller must close
811         default -> {
812            try (var resp = req.run()) { // HTT - exception during close() branch
813               yield materializeResponse(resp, returnMode, returnType, genericReturnType, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback);
814            }
815         }
816         };
817      }
818
819      /** Materializes the terminal value for the buffered return modes (BODY/BEAN/STATUS/NONE) from a run response. */
820      private Object materializeResponse(RestResponse resp, RemoteReturn returnMode, Class<?> returnType, Type genericReturnType, Method method, boolean throwOnError, String acceptFallback) throws Exception {
821         return switch (returnMode) {
822         case BODY -> materializeBufferedBody(resp, returnType, genericReturnType, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback);
823         case BEAN -> {
824            // HTTP-response bean (e.g. Ok / NotFound): materialize from status + body rather than parsing.
825            throwIfError(resp, method, throwOnError);
826            yield instantiateHttpType(returnType, resp.getBodyAsString());
827         }
828         case STATUS -> {
829            var sc = resp.getStatusCode();
830            if (returnType == int.class || returnType == Integer.class)
831               yield sc;
832            if (returnType == boolean.class || returnType == Boolean.class)
833               yield sc < 400;
834            yield sc;
835         }
836         case NONE -> null;
837         case RESPONSE -> throw new IllegalStateException("RESPONSE mode is not a buffered return mode"); // HTT
838         };
839      }
840
841      /** Returns the effective retry count: a positive method-level value overrides the interface-level default. */
842      private int effectiveRetries(RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta methodMeta) {
843         return methodMeta.getRetries() > 0 ? methodMeta.getRetries() : meta.getRetries();
844      }
845
846      /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if the verb may auto-retry: idempotent verbs always, POST/PATCH only when opted in. */
847      private boolean isRetryableVerb(RrpcInterfaceMethodMeta methodMeta) {
848         if (isOneOf(methodMeta.getHttpMethod(), "GET", "PUT", "DELETE", "HEAD"))
849            return true;
850         return methodMeta.isRetryNonIdempotent() || meta.isRetryNonIdempotent();
851      }
852
853      /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if the return mode/type is a buffered (retryable) shape, not a streaming/wrapper one. */
854      private static boolean isRetryableMode(RemoteReturn returnMode, Class<?> returnType) {
855         if (returnMode == RemoteReturn.RESPONSE)
856            return false;
857         if (returnType == InputStream.class || returnType == Reader.class || returnType == Optional.class
858               || returnType == CompletableFuture.class || returnType == Future.class)
859            return false;
860         return ! RecordReader.class.isAssignableFrom(returnType);
861      }
862
863      /** Retryable HTTP statuses: {@code 429} (Too Many Requests) and all {@code 5xx} server errors. */
864      private static boolean isRetryableStatus(int statusCode) {
865         return statusCode == 429 || statusCode >= 500;
866      }
867
868      /** Sleeps a short exponential backoff ({@code 50ms * 2^(attempt-1)}, capped at {@code 1s}) before a retry. */
869      private static void backoff(int attempt) {
870         try {
871            Thread.sleep(Math.min(50L << (attempt - 1), 1000L));
872         } catch (InterruptedException e) {
873            Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
874         }
875      }
876
877      /**
878       * Handles {@link RemoteReturn#BODY} returns, including {@link Optional} and {@link CompletableFuture}/{@link Future}
879       * wrappers and HTTP-response beans.
880       */
881      private Object processBody(RestRequest req, Class<?> returnType, Type genericReturnType, Method method, boolean throwOnError, String acceptFallback) throws Exception {
882         if (returnType == Optional.class) {
883            var inner = innerType(genericReturnType);
884            return o(processBodyValue(req, rawClass(inner), inner, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback));
885         }
886         if (returnType == CompletableFuture.class || returnType == Future.class) {
887            var inner = innerType(genericReturnType);
888            return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(processBodyValue(req, rawClass(inner), inner, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback));
889         }
890         return processBodyValue(req, returnType, genericReturnType, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback);
891      }
892
893      /** Runs the request and materializes a single (unwrapped) body value. */
894      private Object processBodyValue(RestRequest req, Class<?> returnType, Type genericReturnType, Method method, boolean throwOnError, String acceptFallback) throws Exception {
895         // Cursor return types stream over the live response body; the caller owns the cursor (and the response it holds).
896         if (RecordReader.class.isAssignableFrom(returnType))
897            return processCursor(req, returnType, method, throwOnError);
898         // A raw-stream return hands the caller the LIVE response stream — the response must NOT be
899         // closed on success here (the old code returned it from inside try-with-resources, closing it before the
900         // caller could read).  The caller owns the returned stream; closing it releases the connection.
901         if (returnType == InputStream.class)
902            return processStreamReturn(req, method, throwOnError);
903         // A Reader return is a lazy reader over the same live response stream (same lifecycle).
904         if (returnType == Reader.class)
905            return processReaderReturn(req, method, throwOnError);
906         try (var resp = req.run()) { // HTT - exception during close() branch
907            return materializeBufferedBody(resp, returnType, genericReturnType, method, throwOnError, acceptFallback);
908         }
909      }
910
911      /** Materializes a buffered (non-streaming) body value from an already-run response. */
912      private Object materializeBufferedBody(RestResponse resp, Class<?> returnType, Type genericReturnType, Method method, boolean throwOnError, String acceptFallback) throws Exception {
913         throwIfError(resp, method, throwOnError);
914         if (returnType == void.class || returnType == Void.class)
915            return null;
916         if (returnType == String.class)
917            return resp.getBodyAsString();
918         if (returnType == byte[].class)
919            return resp.body().asBytes();
920         if (BasicHttpResponse.class.isAssignableFrom(returnType) || BasicHttpException.class.isAssignableFrom(returnType))
921            return instantiateHttpType(returnType, resp.getBodyAsString());
922         return parseBody(resp, genericReturnType, acceptFallback);
923      }
924
925      /**
926       * Runs the request and hands back the live response body as an {@link InputStream} the caller owns.
927       *
928       * <p>
929       * Mirrors the cursor lifecycle ({@link #processCursor}): on success the response is <b>not</b> closed &mdash; the
930       * returned stream wraps the live response body and closing it releases the connection (closes the body stream and
931       * the response, firing the transport close callback).  On any failure before the stream is handed back, the
932       * response is closed.  A body-less response ({@code null} stream) is released immediately and returns <jk>null</jk>.
933       */
934      @SuppressWarnings({
935         "resource" // On success the live response is owned by the returned stream (caller closes it); on failure it is closed in the finally block.
936      })
937      private InputStream processStreamReturn(RestRequest req, Method method, boolean throwOnError) throws Exception {
938         var resp = req.run();
939         var ok = false;
940         try {
941            throwIfError(resp, method, throwOnError);
942            var stream = resp.getBodyStream();
943            if (stream == null) {
944               ok = true;
945               resp.close();
946               return null;
947            }
948            var result = new ResponseClosingInputStream(stream, resp);
949            ok = true;
950            return result;
951         } finally {
952            if (! ok)
953               resp.close();
954         }
955      }
956
957      /**
958       * Runs the request and hands back the live response body as a {@link Reader} the caller owns.
959       *
960       * <p>
961       * Same lifecycle as {@link #processStreamReturn}: the response is not closed on success; the returned reader wraps
962       * the live response stream (decoded as UTF-8) and closing it releases the connection.
963       */
964      @SuppressWarnings({
965         "resource" // On success the live response is owned by the returned reader (caller closes it); on failure it is closed in the finally block.
966      })
967      private Reader processReaderReturn(RestRequest req, Method method, boolean throwOnError) throws Exception {
968         var resp = req.run();
969         var ok = false;
970         try {
971            throwIfError(resp, method, throwOnError);
972            var stream = resp.getBodyStream();
973            if (stream == null) {
974               ok = true;
975               resp.close();
976               return null;
977            }
978            var result = new ResponseClosingReader(new InputStreamReader(stream, StandardCharsets.UTF_8), resp);
979            ok = true;
980            return result;
981         } finally {
982            if (! ok)
983               resp.close();
984         }
985      }
986
987      /**
988       * A response-body {@link InputStream} that releases the owning {@link RestResponse} when closed.
989       *
990       * <p>
991       * Closing this stream closes the underlying body stream (returning the connection to the pool) and then closes the
992       * {@link RestResponse} (firing the transport close callback), so the connection is released regardless of whether
993       * the transport relies on the body stream or the close callback.
994       */
995      private static final class ResponseClosingInputStream extends FilterInputStream {
996         private final RestResponse response;
997
998         ResponseClosingInputStream(InputStream in, RestResponse response) {
999            super(in);
1000            this.response = response;
1001         }
1002
1003         @Override /* Closeable */
1004         public void close() throws IOException {
1005            try {
1006               super.close();
1007            } finally {
1008               response.close();
1009            }
1010         }
1011
1012         @Override
1013         public int read(byte[] buf, int off, int len) throws IOException {
1014            if (len == 0) return 0;
1015            int b = read();
1016            if (b == -1) return -1;
1017            buf[off] = (byte) b;
1018            return 1;
1019         }
1020      }
1021
1022      /** A response-body {@link Reader} that releases the owning {@link RestResponse} when closed. */
1023      private static final class ResponseClosingReader extends FilterReader {
1024         private final RestResponse response;
1025
1026         ResponseClosingReader(Reader in, RestResponse response) {
1027            super(in);
1028            this.response = response;
1029         }
1030
1031         @Override /* Closeable */
1032         public void close() throws IOException {
1033            try {
1034               super.close();
1035            } finally {
1036               response.close();
1037            }
1038         }
1039      }
1040
1041      /**
1042       * Maps an error HTTP status (&ge;400) to a typed exception declared in the method's {@code throws} clause.
1043       *
1044       * <p>
1045       * A declared exception type matches when it extends {@link BasicHttpException} and its static {@code STATUS_CODE}
1046       * field equals the response status code (e.g. {@code 404} &rarr; {@link NotFound}).
1047       * If no declared type matches, the response is left for normal body handling.
1048       */
1049      private static void throwIfError(RestResponse resp, Method method, boolean throwOnError) throws Exception {
1050         var sc = resp.getStatusCode();
1051         if (sc < 400)
1052            return;
1053         for (var et : method.getExceptionTypes()) {
1054            if (BasicHttpException.class.isAssignableFrom(et) && httpStatusCode(et) == sc)
1055               throw (BasicHttpException) instantiateHttpType(et, resp.getBodyAsString());
1056         }
1057         // No declared exception type matched — if throwOnError is enabled, raise a generic
1058         // HTTP exception carrying the status code and response body rather than returning an error payload.
1059         if (throwOnError)
1060            throw new BasicHttpException(sc, resp.getBodyAsString());
1061      }
1062
1063      /** Returns the static {@code STATUS_CODE} field of an HTTP response/exception type, or {@code -1} if absent. */
1064      private static int httpStatusCode(Class<?> c) {
1065         try {
1066            return c.getField("STATUS_CODE").getInt(null);
1067         } catch (ReflectiveOperationException e) {
1068            return -1;
1069         }
1070      }
1071
1072      /**
1073       * Instantiates an HTTP response/exception bean, preferring a {@code (String body)} constructor and falling back
1074       * to the no-arg constructor (status code is intrinsic to the type).
1075       */
1076      private static Object instantiateHttpType(Class<?> c, String body) {
1077         try {
1078            return newHttpTypeInstance(c, body);
1079         } catch (ReflectiveOperationException e) {
1080            throw rex(e, "Could not instantiate HTTP response/exception type {0}", c.getName());
1081         }
1082      }
1083
1084      /** Instantiates {@code c} preferring a {@code (String body)} constructor, falling back to the no-arg constructor. */
1085      private static Object newHttpTypeInstance(Class<?> c, String body) throws ReflectiveOperationException {
1086         try {
1087            return c.getConstructor(String.class).newInstance(body);
1088         } catch (NoSuchMethodException e) {
1089            return c.getConstructor().newInstance();
1090         }
1091      }
1092
1093      /** Returns the raw {@link Class} of a possibly-parameterized type. */
1094      private static Class<?> rawClass(Type t) {
1095         if (t instanceof Class<?> c)
1096            return c;
1097         if (t instanceof ParameterizedType p)
1098            return (Class<?>) p.getRawType();
1099         return Object.class;
1100      }
1101
1102      /** Returns the first type argument of a parameterized wrapper (e.g. {@code Optional<T>} &rarr; {@code T}). */
1103      private static Type innerType(Type t) {
1104         if (t instanceof ParameterizedType p) {
1105            var args = p.getActualTypeArguments();
1106            if (args.length > 0)
1107               return args[0];
1108         }
1109         return Object.class;
1110      }
1111
1112      private static RestRequest withContentBody(RestRequest req, Object arg, BodyFormat bodyFormat) throws IOException {
1113         if (arg instanceof HttpBody b)
1114            return req.body(b);
1115         if (arg instanceof String s)
1116            return req.bodyString(s);
1117         // Stream the Reader straight to the wire instead of draining it into a String first.
1118         if (arg instanceof Reader r)
1119            return req.body(ReaderBody.of(r));
1120         // When a contentType attribute is in effect, serialize the POJO with the selected serializer
1121         // and send the overridden Content-Type label (replacing the serializer's default — exactly one header).
1122         if (bodyFormat.isSet())
1123            return req.body(SerializerBody.of(bodyFormat.serializer(), arg, bodyFormat.contentType()));
1124         return req.body(arg);
1125      }
1126
1127      /**
1128       * Assembles a {@code multipart/form-data} request body from the method's {@link Part @Part} parameters.
1129       *
1130       * <p>
1131       * A thin adapter onto the existing streaming {@link MultipartBody}: each non-<jk>null</jk> {@code @Part}
1132       * argument becomes one part whose name/filename/content-type come from the annotation and whose body is mapped
1133       * from the argument type by {@link #toPartBody(Object, String)} (file/stream/reader/byte[] parts stream, reusing
1134       * the streaming bodies; beans are serialized).  The {@code multipart/form-data} {@code Content-Type} (with
1135       * its boundary) is supplied by {@link MultipartBody#getContentType()} when the request is built.
1136       */
1137      private void bindMultipartBody(RestRequest req, Method method, Object[] args) {
1138         var builder = MultipartBody.builder();
1139         var params = method.getParameters();
1140         for (var i = 0; i < params.length; i++) {
1141            var part = params[i].getAnnotation(Part.class);
1142            if (part == null)
1143               continue;
1144            var arg = (args == null || i >= args.length) ? null : args[i];
1145            if (arg != null) {
1146               var name = firstNonEmpty(part.name(), part.value(), params[i].getName());
1147               var contentType = part.contentType().isEmpty() ? null : part.contentType();
1148               var fileName = firstNonEmpty(part.fileName().isEmpty() ? null : part.fileName(), defaultFileName(arg));
1149               builder.part(MultipartBody.MultipartPart.of(name, fileName, contentType, toPartBody(arg, contentType)));
1150            }
1151         }
1152         req.body(builder.build());
1153      }
1154
1155      /**
1156       * Maps a {@code @Part} argument to a streaming-or-buffered {@link HttpBody} based on its runtime type.
1157       *
1158       * <p>
1159       * {@link File}/{@link InputStream}/{@link Reader} and {@code byte[]} parts reuse the streaming bodies
1160       * (never pre-buffered); scalar values become a text {@link StringBody}; an {@link HttpBody} is used directly;
1161       * any other object (a bean) is streamed through the client's default {@link Serializer}.
1162       */
1163      private HttpBody toPartBody(Object arg, String contentType) {
1164         if (arg instanceof HttpBody b)
1165            return b;
1166         if (arg instanceof byte[] bytes)
1167            return ByteArrayBody.of(bytes, firstNonEmpty(contentType, MEDIA_OCTET_STREAM));
1168         if (arg instanceof File f)
1169            return FileBody.of(f, firstNonEmpty(contentType, MEDIA_OCTET_STREAM));
1170         if (arg instanceof InputStream in)
1171            return StreamBody.of(in, firstNonEmpty(contentType, MEDIA_OCTET_STREAM));
1172         if (arg instanceof Reader r)
1173            return ReaderBody.of(r, firstNonEmpty(contentType, "text/plain; charset=UTF-8"));
1174         if (isScalarPart(arg))
1175            return StringBody.of(arg.toString(), firstNonEmpty(contentType, "text/plain; charset=UTF-8"));
1176         // Bean part: stream it through the client's default serializer (no full in-memory materialization).
1177         var s = client.getDefaultSerializer().orElseThrow(() -> new IllegalStateException(
1178            "No default serializer is configured on the client.  Configure one via RestClient.Builder.defaultSerializer(...)."));
1179         return contentType != null ? SerializerBody.of(s, arg, contentType) : SerializerBody.of(s, arg);
1180      }
1181
1182      /** Returns the default filename for a {@code @Part} argument: a {@link File}'s own name, else <jk>null</jk>. */
1183      private static String defaultFileName(Object arg) {
1184         return arg instanceof File f ? f.getName() : null;
1185      }
1186
1187      /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if the {@code @Part} argument is a scalar that should be sent as a text field. */
1188      private static boolean isScalarPart(Object arg) {
1189         return arg instanceof CharSequence || arg instanceof Number || arg instanceof Boolean
1190            || arg instanceof Character || arg instanceof Enum || arg instanceof Date;
1191      }
1192
1193      /**
1194       * Runs the request and opens a token/record-streaming cursor over the (live) response body.
1195       *
1196       * <p>
1197       * Unlike the buffered body paths, the response is <b>not</b> closed here on success: the returned cursor reads
1198       * directly from the response stream and the caller owns it (close the cursor when done).  On any failure before
1199       * the cursor is handed back, the response is closed.
1200       */
1201      @SuppressWarnings({
1202         "resource" // On success the response is owned by the returned cursor (caller closes it); on failure it is closed in the finally block.
1203      })
1204      private Object processCursor(RestRequest req, Class<?> returnType, Method method, boolean throwOnError) throws Exception {
1205         var resp = req.run();
1206         var ok = false;
1207         try {
1208            throwIfError(resp, method, throwOnError);
1209            var cursor = resp.body().asCursor(returnType);
1210            ok = true;
1211            return cursor;
1212         } finally {
1213            if (! ok)
1214               resp.close();
1215         }
1216      }
1217
1218      /**
1219       * Parses a response body for an {@code @Remote}-proxy method return value.
1220       *
1221       * <p>
1222       * Unlike {@link ResponseBody#as(Parser, Class)},
1223       * which always surfaces a parse failure as an {@code IOException}, this method applies a deliberate proxy-only
1224       * leniency: when the declared return type is {@code Object}, a {@link ParseException} is swallowed and the raw
1225       * response body string is returned as-is. For any other declared return type the {@code ParseException}
1226       * propagates unchanged.
1227       *
1228       * <p>
1229       * Response parsing remains driven by the response {@code Content-Type} (the server is
1230       * authoritative about what it actually sent).  The {@code acceptFallback} media type is consulted only when the
1231       * response is unlabeled or its {@code Content-Type} matches no registered parser (see {@link #selectParser}).
1232       */
1233      private static Object parseBody(RestResponse resp, Type returnType, String acceptFallback) throws Exception {
1234         var body = resp.getBodyAsString();
1235         if (body == null)
1236            return null;
1237         var h = resp.getFirstHeader("Content-Type");
1238         var parser = selectParser(resp, h == null ? null : h.value(), acceptFallback);
1239         try {
1240            return parser.parse(body, returnType);
1241         } catch (ParseException e) {
1242            if (returnType == Object.class)
1243               return body;
1244            throw e;
1245         }
1246      }
1247
1248      /**
1249       * Selects the response parser honoring the locked precedence: the response {@code Content-Type}
1250       * is authoritative; the {@code accept} media type is only a fallback.
1251       *
1252       * <ol>
1253       *    <li>If the response {@code Content-Type} matches a registered parser, use it.
1254       *    <li>Otherwise (response unlabeled or its type matched nothing), use the parser matching the {@code accept}
1255       *       media type, if one is registered.
1256       *    <li>Otherwise fall back to the client's explicitly-configured default parser
1257       *       ({@link RestClient#getMatchingParser(String)}); if none is configured this throws
1258       *       <c>415 Unsupported Media Type</c> (there is no implicit JSON fallback).
1259       * </ol>
1260       *
1261       * @param resp The response (source of the client + parsers).
1262       * @param responseContentType The response {@code Content-Type} header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
1263       * @param acceptFallback The {@code accept} media type fallback. May be <jk>null</jk>/empty.
1264       * @return The parser to use. Never <jk>null</jk>.
1265       * @throws UnsupportedMediaType If no parser matches and no default parser is configured on the client.
1266       */
1267      private static Parser selectParser(RestResponse resp, String responseContentType, String acceptFallback) {
1268         var c = resp.getClient();
1269         var p = c.getParserForMediaType(responseContentType);
1270         if (p.isPresent())
1271            return p.get();
1272         if (isNotEmpty(acceptFallback)) {
1273            var ap = c.getParserForMediaType(acceptFallback);
1274            if (ap.isPresent())
1275               return ap.get();
1276         }
1277         return c.getMatchingParser(responseContentType).orElseThrow(() -> new UnsupportedMediaType(
1278            "No parser matched the response Content-Type ''{0}'' and no default parser is configured on the client.", responseContentType));
1279      }
1280
1281      private static String combinePaths(String base, String method) {
1282         if (base.isEmpty())
1283            return method.isEmpty() ? "" : method;
1284         if (method.isEmpty())
1285            return base;
1286         // Avoid double slashes
1287         if (base.endsWith("/") && method.startsWith("/"))
1288            return base + method.substring(1);
1289         if (!base.endsWith("/") && !method.startsWith("/"))
1290            return base + "/" + method;
1291         return base + method;
1292      }
1293   }
1294}
